title: Pathfinding Update and Cluster Management
system_overview: Two parallel pathfinding systems: (1) Pathfinder – Dijkstra/flow-field, used by AIController.pathfind() for wave AI. (2) ControlPathfinder – HPA* cluster-based, runs on a background thread at 30 fps, used by CommandAI.getPathPosition() for player-commanded units. Both are updated when tiles change.
interaction_flow: --- CONTROL PATHFINDER EVENT REGISTRATION (checkEvents, done once) ---
Events.on(WorldLoadEvent) → controlPath.stop(); controlPath = new ControlPathfinder(); controlPath.start()
ControlPathfinder.updateSingleTile(t) → compute cx/cy/cluster from tile coords
[IF tile on cluster edge] → queueClusterUpdate(cx±1, cy) and/or queueClusterUpdate(cx, cy±1) + queueClusterUpdate(cx,cy)
1. queue.run()  // drains TaskQueue lambdas (populates clustersToUpdate / clustersToInnerUpdate)
5. [every 1000 ms] iterate invalidRequests → if field.frontier.isEmpty(): fields.remove(field.mapKey); queue.post(recalculatePath(request))
updateClustersComplete(clusterIndex) → for each [team][costType]: updateCluster(team, pathCost, cx, cy)
→ for each of 4 directions: scan border tiles, detect passable spans → create/share portals (IntSeq) with neighbor cluster
→ for each pair of portals in cluster: run innerAstar(minX,minY,maxX,maxY, portalA, portalB) → connectionCost
→ if reachable: add IntraEdge to cluster.portalConnections[dir][i] (bidirectional)
updateClustersInner(clusterIndex) → for each [team][pathCost]: updateInnerEdges only (portals unchanged)
ControlPathfinder.getCreateCluster(team, pathCost, cx, cy) → getCluster() → if null: updateCluster(team, pathCost, cx, cy)
updateFields(FieldCache, nsToRun) → BFS from goalPos outward through cluster graph, populating FieldCache.fields (clusterIndex→int[]) with cost values, time-bounded by nsToRun
→ raycast(team, cost, tileX, tileY, destX, destY): if LOS clear → out.set(destination); return true
→ request = unitRequests.get(unit)  [create PathRequest if missing: new PathRequest(unit,team,costId,destPos)]
→ traverse fieldCache to find next tile (lowest cost neighbor, up to 30 iterations)
Pathfinder.getField(team, costType, fieldType) → if cache miss: create Flowfield, queue.post(registerPath(field)); return field
Flowfield.getNextTile(tile, unitAvoidanceId) → scan d4/d8 neighbors; return tile with lowest weights[] value
key_data_structures: Cluster: portals[4] (IntSeq[] of packed [from,to] ranges), portalConnections[4][] (LongSeq of IntraEdge)
FieldCache: fields (IntMap<int[]> clusterIdx→costArray), frontier (IntQueue for BFS), goalPos/costId/team, mapKey (FieldIndex long)
PathRequest: unit, destination (packed tile pos), costId, team, resultPath (IntSeq), costs/cameFrom/frontier for A*, lastRaycastTile/Result
FieldIndex (long): packed (pos|costId|team) – key into ControlPathfinder.fields map
NodeIndex (int): packed (cluster|dir|portal) – node in HPA* abstract graph
key_relationships: Tile fires TileChangeEvent → ControlPathfinder.updateTile() (via Events listener)
ControlPathfinder implements Runnable; background thread at MIN_PRIORITY, 30fps
ControlPathfinder owns unitRequests (Unit→PathRequest, MAIN THREAD ONLY)
ControlPathfinder owns fields (long→FieldCache, written on pathfinding thread)
ControlPathfinder.queue (TaskQueue) bridges main thread → pathfinding thread
AIController.pathfind() uses Pathfinder (old flow-field) NOT ControlPathfinder
GroundAI extends AIController; calls pathfind(Pathfinder.fieldCore) for wave enemy movement
cost_types: costGround (id=0): avoids deep tiles, liquid-penalty, enemy walls cost 1M
costHover (id=1): same as ground but ignores liquids/deep
costNaval (id=3): prefers liquid tiles, avoids non-liquid (cost 6000)
threading_notes: clustersToUpdate / clustersToInnerUpdate: ONLY modified on pathfinding thread (posted via TaskQueue)
fields (LongMap<FieldCache>): written on pathfinding thread, READ on main thread (marked 'very dangerous')
PathRequest.oldCache: volatile reference used to avoid unit stuttering during recalculation
maxUpdate = 12ms per iteration for updateFields; thread sleeps 33ms between iterations
important_notes: ControlPathfinder is HPA* (Hierarchical Pathfinding A*) with 12x12 tile clusters. Each cluster has portals (passable spans on borders) and intra-edges (A* costs between portal pairs). Flow fields are computed per (goalPos, costId, team) triplet via BFS outward from goal. The old Pathfinder (Dijkstra flow field, resolution-based) co-exists and is used by wave/basic AI. ControlPathfinder is used by CommandAI (player-commanded units) and LogicAI.
